text.title: Vision Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every VLM extraction call must enforce. Source: OpenAI vision docs, Anthropic vision guide, Gemini Vision pricing, production replay logs.

rule.statement: Resize every image to 1024 pixels on the long edge before encoding. Use Pillow's `Image.thumbnail((1024, 1024), Image.LANCZOS)`. Reject untouched 4K source images.

rationale.source: OpenAI vision cost calculator: 1024px ~525 tokens, 2048px ~1700 tokens

rule.rationale: VLMs bill by tile count which scales with resolution. 1024px keeps text and screenshots fully legible while cutting token cost by 50-70%.

rule.statement: For sensitive content (PII, internal docs, customer screenshots), send as base64 (inline in the request). For public references (CDN URLs, public datasets), URL input is acceptable.

rationale.source: Provider URL-fetch logging behaviour; GDPR + SOC-2 audit findings

rule.rationale: Providers that accept URLs retrieve from the URL host, which may be logged, cached, or proxied externally. Base64 keeps the image bytes inside the API call, simplifying compliance posture.

rule.statement: When passing >1 image in one prompt, prefix every image with an explicit label (`Image 1:`, `Image 2:`). Reference labels in the task instructions.

rationale.source: Multi-image comparison drift on unlabeled inputs (Claude + GPT-4o)

rule.rationale: Without labels, VLMs conflate images or describe them out of order. Explicit numbering anchors each reference in the response.

rule.statement: For OpenAI, set `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` AND include the JSON schema in the prompt. For Claude, include the Pydantic JSON schema in the prompt and use prefill `{` to bias toward JSON. Always parse with try/except and re-prompt on failure.

rationale.source: Schema-drift incidents under production load (OpenAI + Anthropic)

rule.rationale: json_object mode prevents prose-before-JSON but does not guarantee schema adherence. Including the schema in the prompt + retry-on-parse is the empirical sweet spot.

rule.statement: On `json.JSONDecodeError` OR `pydantic.ValidationError`, re-prompt with the specific error message and the schema. Cap at 2 retries; on third failure return status="error".

rationale.source: Single-retry success rate ~85% on edge-case extractions

rule.rationale: Even with json_object mode, edge cases produce malformed JSON. A targeted re-prompt with the error message recovers most cases within one retry.

rule.statement: Cache extraction results keyed on `sha256(image_bytes + task_prompt + provider + model)`. NOT on filename. The cache lives at `{cache_dir}/{key}.json`.

rationale.source: Pipeline replay cost analysis: identical images rescanned 3-5x per week

rule.rationale: Filenames are reused across pipeline runs (e.g., `screenshot.png`). A bytes-hash key is stable regardless of how the file is named or moved.
